Alternative Solution proposal - building will include a seasonal enclosure to the existing tier of seating, (i.e., bowl) that will allow the RBC Amphiteatre to accommodate concerts during winter months. The requirements of the OBC with respect to Group A, Division 4 occupancies is intended for occupants gatering in oper air seating areas.  As discussed above, the intent of the design is to maintain the current Group A, Division 4 major occupancy, even though the building will be enclosed at certain seasons.  Therefore, to maintain the same major occupancy classification of A4 for the enclosed bowl area at certain seasons, it is proposed to provide active mechanical ventilation and natural ventilation (smoke control system) for the seating area and egress paths. Seating and egress areas have been designed based on A4 requirements, and since smoke protection is implemented for the enclosed bowl areas, the same A4 occupancy classification can be maintained within seasonal enclosure of the Bowl. In accordance with Article 3.3.2.11 every seat is located so that the travel distance is not more than 45 m measured along the path of travel from the seat to an opening to a passageway leading from the seating area. Majority of these passageways leads to an exit through the west and east concourses. Since the East and West concourses will be mostly open to exterior and partially covered by a roof these spaces are deemed to be an extensions of the Bowl. OBC does not contain requirements pertaining to travel distances within these spaces. Therefore, the analysis of smoke layer and egress paths (including egress time) utilizing computer modeling is proposed. The smoke and egress modeling determined that occupants could leave the building prior to the smoke layer descending and affecting their egress. In accordance with Sentence 3.2.2.11(3)  Exits from outdoor stadia or grandstands shall be located not more than 25 m apart.  The amphitheater is located on an island; therefore, it is not possible to meet the requirement of Sentence (3). Considering proposed measures the proposed design including seasonal enclosure meets the intent of the Code with respect to egress and exits requirements pertaining to outdoor places of assembly occupancy, and as such, the proposed design is acceptable.

Site Plan Application to permit a new office building at 39-49 Fraser Avenue on the western portion of the property facing Fraser Avenue. The proposal includes conserving the existing heritage buildings on Fraser Avenue and adding office floors above, to a total height of 10 storeys with a non-residential gross floor area of 14,268 m2.A Rezoning Application (21 160624 STE 10 OZ) for the phased redevelopment of two office buildings is under review. Two separate site plan applications have been submitted for review as the development is a phased project. Phase 1 will include  bicycle and loading spaces that will be shared with the development in Phase 2. A concurrent site plan application is under review for Phase 2(21 251000 STE 10 SA) to permit a new 11-storey office building along Pardee Avenue.

Site Plan Application to permit Phase 2 of development for a new office building at 53 Fraser Avenue and 8-42 Pardee Avenue on the eastern portion of the property facing Pardee Avenue. The proposal includes conserving the existing heritage building at 8 Pardee Avenue and adding a new 11-storey office building with a total non-residential gross floor area of 18,029 m2 and retail use of 551 m2. Phase 1 will include  bicycle and loading spaces that will be shared with the development in Phase 2.A Rezoning Application (21 160624 STE 10 OZ) for the phased redevelopment of two office buildings is under review. Two separate site plan applications have been submitted for review as the development is a phased project.  A concurrent site plan application is under review for Phase 1 (21 250 999 STE 10 SA) to permit a new 10-storey office building along Fraser Avenue.

Construct a 48 storey residential building. One level of underground parking. 517 units, approx 30,512 m2 gross floor area.  The first of the two development sites is located at the southeast corner of Weston Road and Locust  Street (the West Site). The West Site is comprised of the properties unicipally known as 1175-1181 Weston Road and has a combined site area of approximately 1,143 square metres (0.114 hectares). The second of the two development sites is located further east of the West Site on the south side of Locust Street (the ¿East Site¿). The East Site is comprised of the properties municipally known as 7-17 Locust Street and has a combined area of approximately 1,482 square metres (0.148 hectares). The West Site and the East Site are physically separated by a T shaped parcel of land known municipally as 1169 Weston Road that is owned and operated by the Toronto Parking Authority as a surface parking lot (the TPA Lands).

INACTIVE Site Plan Control application to permit the proposed Humber Cultural Hub which is a phased institutional development as part of the Humber College Lakeshore Campus. The proposal includes the partial redevelopment of the existing building with the construction of multiple building additions that would be developed in two phases. The first phase would include the construction of an 8 student residence building that would be connected by 1 to 4-storey academic podiums. The second phase would include a new 3-storey academic podium, with a music and performing arts hall, which would connect to the first phase. The existing 6-storey student residence fronting Lake Shore Boulevard West would remain, as well as the existing 2 and 3-storey library building which would connect to the proposed development. The existing 2-storey academic building component located in the center of the site would be demolished and a portion of the existing surface parking lot would be removed to facilitate the proposal.The total gross floor area of the institutional development would be 49,611 m², of which 30,380 m² would be attributed to the proposed development and 19,231 m² would be attributed to the existing buildings that would be retained on the site.

Proposed Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendments for Quayside Master Plan Blocks 3b, 4 and 5. The proposal for Blocks 3b, 4 and 5 includes two residential towers and one mid-rise building, collectively accommodating 8,988 square metres of non-residential Gross Floor Area (GFA) and 119,581 square metres of residential GFA. The residential component includes 1,475 condo units and 407 affordable units. The buildings will flank a portion of a 14,350 square metre east-west "Community Forest" which is continuous through the centre of the broader development. The mid-rise building on Block 3B will also support portion of a rooftop urban farm, which extends the length of the mid-rise buildings on Blocks 1, 2 and 3b connected by a suspended pedestrian bridge across Small Street.  An application is required to amend the City of Toronto Official Plan to permit additional height and density and an application to amend the former City of Toronto Zoning By-law 438-86, as amended by OMB-approved By-law 1174-2010, is required to permit additional height and density, amend the permitted uses, update zoning maps and revise certain zoning standards to permit the proposed development. This application forms part of the broader Quayside Master Plan, which is comprised of seven mixed use buildings on four development blocks and public realm improvements on the interior and perimiter of the site. The development includes 321,760 square metres of GFA allotted as 22,562 square metres of commercial, retail, institutional and other non-residential uses and 299,209 square metres of residential uses supporting 4,634 condo units and 869 affordable housing units. Public realm and infrastructure works to be delivered by Waterfront Toronto include the Queens Quay East extension, plazas at Parliament Street and Queens Quay East, an extended Water's Edge Promenade and a 0.4 hectare park.

Proposal includes three buildings. Building "A": A third floor addition to the existing 2-storey building. Building "B": The existing three-storey building (1,387 m2) to remain with interior modifications and renovations only. The first floor will continue to house a synagogue and administrative functions, while the second and third floors will be converted into dormitory accommodations. Building "C": A new three-storey addition that will be constructed and integrated with Building A. The basement level will feature a cafeteria, while the first and second floors will be dedicated to classrooms and offices. The third floor will connect with the newly constructed third floor of Building A to form a dormitory space and additional classrooms.

Construction of a 3-storey building for water treatment process upgrades, including ultraviolet (UV) disinfection and a residuals management facility (RMF). There are varying rooftop heights ranging from approximately 8 m to 24 m above grade. There will be 3 new parking spaces available in the drive area. The height of the building is approximately 3.9 m above grade. To permit the development of a  21-storey building (Building A on the north-east corner of Lawrence Avenue East and Birchmount Road, an 8-storey mid-rise building (Building B) along the Birchmount Road frontage, a 7-storey mid-rise building (Building C), and a 6-storey mid-rise building (Building D) internal to the site. Additionally, a new public park is proposed along the Dulverton Road frontage having proposed size of 948 square metres. A previous Zoning By-law Amendment Application for the subject property was submitted in 2017, culminating with the Council enactment of By-laws 1137-2018 and 1139-2018 on July 23rd, 2018, permitting the development of 286 residential units in the form of a 21 and seven (7) storey apartment building and three (3) free standing, 3.5 storey townhouse buildings. On July 16, 2021, By-laws 632/633-2021 were approved to amend the original application by increasing the residential unit count from 286 units to 429 units in a similar building massing of apartment and townhouse units. The following refinements/amendments to the currently approved proposed development are as follows: 1. Adjustments to Buildings B, C and D (Building Heights from 7 storeys to 8 storeys for Building B, 3.5 storeys to 7 storeys to Building C and 3.5 storeys to 6 storeys for Building D) of Site-Specific By-laws 1137-2018/1138-2018 and 632-2021/633-2021. 2.To increase the overall number of residential suites from 429 to 488 (to be facilitated via the increased heights to Buildings B, C and D). 3. Modify the overall residential gross floor area from 24,000.00 square metres to approximately 27,000.00 square metres. No changes or reconstruction of any sort is being proposed to Building A.

Site Plan proposal for the first phase of development for the Victory Silos Site North Block, bounded by Lake Shore Boulevard East to the north and the planned extensions of Parliament Street, Queens Quay East and Trinity Street to the west, south and east, respectively. The North Block is approximately 3,690 square metres in size and will be developed with a 41-storey mixed-use building comprised of a 11-storey podium 2 building and a 30-storey tower element located at the west end of the block. The height of the building is 130 metres, excluding the mechanical penthouse. The building has a total gross floor area of approximately 46,316 square metres, including approximately 44,975 square metres of residential gross floor area and approximately 1,341 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, which results in an overall density of 12.55 FSI. The development consists of a total of 691 units: 42 studio units, 339 1-bedroom units, 241 2-bedroom units and 69 3-bedroom units. A total of 227 automobile parking spaces (165 spaces for residents, 44 spaces for visitors and 18 spaces for retail uses) and a total of 704 bicycle parking spaces (633 long-terms spaces and 71 short-term spaces) are provided in the building, as well as a Type G loading space and a Type C loading space. Approximately 804 square metres of interior amenity space and approximately 1,030 square metres of outdoor amenity space is provided. Related subdivision application: 21 233775 STE 10 SB

Construct a 38 storey mixed-use building with retail at grade, residential above, and one level of underground parking. 382 units, approx gross floor area 22,576m2. Associated with 17 Locust Street.The first of the two development sites is located at the southeast corner of Weston Road and Locust Street (the West Site). The West Site is comprised of the properties municipally known as 1175-1181 Weston Road and has a combined site area of approximately 1,143 square metres (0.114 hectares). The second of the two development sites is located further east of the West Site on the south side of Locust Street (the East Site). The East Site is comprised of the properties municipally known as 7-17 Locust Street and has a combined area of approximately 1,482 square metres (0.148 hectares). The West Site and the East Site are physically separated by a T shaped parcel of land known municipally as 1169 Weston Road that is owned and operated by the Toronto Parking Authority as a surface parking lot (the TPA Lands).

The application proposes to amend the previous development concept to introduce an additional tower, for a total of four, along the northern half of the site, preserving the mid-rise built form fronting  Queens Quay East. The proposed development increases the GFA by 9,230 square metres (m2) (4.8%) to a total of 202,878 m2, comprised of 197,396 m2 of residential GFA and 5,482 m2 of non-residential uses. The application also proposes 45,645 square metres of affordable rental housing GFA (23% of overall site GFA). Approximately 2,850 total residential units are proposed (a 39 unit increase), including 1,129 purpose built rental units and 553 affordable rental units (a 95 unit increase), as well as a 4,100 m2 POPS, a 3,060 m2 community hub providing 62 child care spaces and other community uses, and 2,231 m2 of retail.

Site plan application for Quayside Building 2, a 53 storey, 576 unit building to be delivered as part of the Quayside project. The proposed development includes a total GFA of 40,887 square metres (m2), comprised of 37,636 m2 of residential and 3,251 m2 of non-residential uses. The non-residential use is 3,251 m2 for a Community Hub that will provide a range of community uses, including a 62-child private daycare. A TTC substation is also provided to service the Waterfront East LRT.
